political.s: Toyota Motor North America operates the Toyota/Lexus PAC which makes donations to federal candidates and parties. Available FEC data shows Toyota maintains a registered PAC for political contributions and lobbying activities.

charity.s: Lexus and Toyota have raised over 430 million dollars for charities and work with more than 400 nonprofit organizations. The Lexus Pursuit of Potential program focuses on children in need, with dealer matching funds generating millions in annual contributions.
charity.focus: Children in need, education, STEM, disaster relief, community development

environment.s: Toyota North America achieved a 32% reduction in Scope 1&2 emissions versus 2019 and reports 83% of Toyota and Lexus models now have electrified options. The company maintains science-based targets validated by SBTi and achieved a 15% water reduction in FY2025.

labor.s: Toyota has faced multiple labor challenges including worker organizing concerns at US plants, allegations of overwork in domestic and overseas operations, and a penalty total of $9.1 billion since 2000 across all violations. The company maintains a non-union workforce at its US production facilities despite UAW organizing efforts.

dei.s: Toyota USA previously promoted DEI initiatives and was the No. 1 automaker in multicultural markets, but announced in October 2024 it would narrow community activities and withdraw from participation in the Human Rights Campaign's Corporate Equality Index following external backlash.

animals.s: Not applicable to luxury automotive manufacturer.

guns.s: Not applicable to automotive manufacturer; does not manufacture or sell firearms.

privacy.s: Limited publicly available information on Lexus USA data privacy practices. As a subsidiary of Toyota Motor Corporation, privacy policies align with parent company standards but specific independent privacy ratings are not readily available.

execPay.s: Toyota parent company Chairman Akio Toyoda received record compensation of ¥1.622 billion (~$10.09 million) in 2024, while average worker compensation data is not publicly disclosed with corresponding ratio. Executive pay appears elevated relative to industry and worker compensation.
